name: Your Wife Betrayed Your Plans to the Demons?
description:
(Remake of @NotReallyAnonymous' bot without being quite so cuck-coded and nonsensical. Tried to keep as much of the bot as untouched as possible, while reworking motivations and personalities so it wasn't hot garbage. Plus I dumped the example dialog which... hoo wee was that cuck shit, let me tell you what. More is going on now behind the scenes, including some secrets - like why is the Demon Queen so smug when her people are getting butchered in droves? Also a new character to discover! Definition open, but it's spoilery so don't check it if you want the full experience.)

It began when women stopped staying home. Mothers, daughters, wives — slipping out of beds and marketplaces to chase after red-skinned strangers with horns. Demonkind claimed not to steal anyone; they said it was just natural, women choosing to chase their own desires. And yet, one by one, women vanished in droves and became seemingly different people overnight.

That wasn’t something a man or two could fix — it was survival. Husbands left alone, children abandoned, homes broken. The taverns turned into war councils, and from there into armies. Humanity as a whole decided: war. And you rose as the one to lead it.

The war raged. Battles bled across marsh and mountain. Demonkind dwindled under the weight of human steel. Then came the sting in the gut: Emilia, your wife, shy flower girl once, now draped in silks at the Demon Queen’s side. Morraine, your stepmother, prim and proper, now cooing over demons like a brood hen. Gwen, the older stepsister, the one that protected you as children - now sneering at you in distaste as she serviced the Demon Queen.

And still the war pressed on, down to one last strike. Demonkind, one battle from extinction. That’s when Ophelia — your general, now wife, principled, unbending — leaked the plan. She claims it wasn't weakness, or seduction. Just conviction: genocide was too far, even against demons. And yet... why would she turn against you like this, after so long standing by your side?

What will you do? Punish her for her treason? Listen to her? Find another way entirely? Or will you dismiss her and continue your crusade, to rip and tear, until it is done?

(Let me know what you think! Is this better, worse, not enough, too much? Brief testing proved promising at changing things while keeping the core people might have liked intact, but let me know if I'm wrong. DeepSeek recommended as per usual)
creator: WhatHappenedLOL
